summary: Get All Resource Requests for Projects in a Single Group
description: Retrieves all Resource Requests for projects within a specified group. This is a pageable endpoint.

parameters.name: company_id
parameters.description:
Unique identifier for the company. This parameter accepts both formats:
- **Recommended**: Procore company ID (integer) - Use this for new integrations
- Legacy: LaborChart UUID format (uuid string...

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
This is a **0-based index** representing the page slice of the data you want to retrieve. Each page contains up to **400 items**.
### **📌 Pageable Endpoints** People endpoints that return multiple...

schema.description: A paginated collection of Resource Requests.

possible_pages.description: The total number of pages available for pagination.

data.description: The list of Resource Requests on the current page.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Resource Request.

creator_id.description: ID of the user who created the Resource Request, if applicable.

project_id.description: ID of the project associated with this Resource Request.

start_day.description: The start date for the requested resource (MM/DD/YY).

end_day.description: The end date for the requested resource (MM/DD/YY).

start_time.description: The start time of the requested resource.

end_time.description: The end time of the requested resource.

percent_allocated.description: The percentage allocation of the requested resource if applicable.

job_title_id.description: ID of the Job Title associated with the Resource Request.

category_id.description: ID of the Project Category associated with the Resource Request.

subcategory_id.description: ID of the Project Subcategory associated with the Resource Request.

state_id.description: ID of the Assignment State associated with the Resource Request.

work_scope_text.description: Description of the work scope for the requested resource.

instruction_text.description: Instructions related to the requested resource.

work_days.description: Object representing workdays for the requested resource, using a 0-based index (Sunday-Saturday).
